results.possibleIdentity: Emery Heber (active late 1950s–1970s) was an electronics engineer associated with Bell Telephone Laboratories (Bell Labs). He is known to have been an inventor at Bell Labs, notably holding US Patent 2,813,200, titled "Magnetically controlled gas discharge device," which was issued in 1957. He resided or was professionally based in West Orange, New Jersey. During the late 1960s and early 1970s, Heber was involved with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization that facilitated collaborations between artists and the technical community. His technical expertise in electronics led to his inclusion in the E.A.T. Technical Services program, which matched engineers from institutions like Bell Labs with artists seeking technical assistance for their projects.
